title: Buildx Builders, Multi-Platform Images, Bake, and OCI Outputs · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Buildx Builders Drivers
summary: A CLI release must support linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 without silently shipping an emulated, untested, or single-platform image. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker buildx builders drivers increment in multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities.
Control immutable inputs, time, order, package resolution, frontend and BuildKit compatibility, platform, metadata, and comparison while documenting unavoidable variance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Buildx Builders Drivers · Theory Docker Buildx Builders Drivers
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Buildx Builders Drivers contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification. Competency probe: Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities. A defensible solution applies Docker Buildx Builders Drivers, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-buildx-builders-drivers — Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-multiplatform-buildx-bake-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-multiplatform-buildx-bake-3
    artifact: docker-bake.hcl
    decision: worked docker multiplatform buildx bake 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      target "release" {
      platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
      attest = ["type=provenance", "type=sbom"]
      output = ["type=registry"]
      }
    verify: docker buildx bake --print release && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate
    failure: Reject architecture emulation as native test evidence, local image load for a multi-platform index, or platform-dependent inputs without declarations.
    repair: Inspect builder capabilities, make target platform explicit, test each artifact, publish the index, and verify per-platform digest selection.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Every Buildx builder shares the daemon image store, cache, platforms, and outputs. This fails the Docker Buildx Builders Drivers contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
